item.title: CMA Consultation on Mobile App Steering and NFC Access 摘要
item.description:
The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is consulting on mobile app 'steering'—the ability for developers to direct customers to off-platform payment options. Currently, Apple bans steering and Google restricts it in the UK. Lifting these constraints would let developers bypass mandatory platform fees. The CMA proposes principles for fair and reasonable steering fees, expected to be lower than current app store charges, with savings passed to UK customers or reinvested in developer innovation. Japan's MSCA is cited as a good model for such regulations.
标签: CMA, UK, Apple, Google, app store, steering, antitrust, regulation, mobile, fees

item.title: Have your agent record video demos of its work with shot-scraper video 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison announces shot-scraper 1.10, which introduces a new 'video' command that accepts a storyboard.yml file to define a routine against a web application and records a video using Playwright. The post demonstrates a comprehensive example exercising a Datasette feature for creating tables from pasted CSV/TSV/JSON data. The YAML storyboard was entirely generated by GPT-5.5 xhigh in Codex Desktop, following a prompt that included reviewing branch changes and running the tool's --help output. Willison highlights the pattern of bundling detailed help text so coding agents can autonomously use the tool, similar to his previous work on showboat and rodney.
标签: shot-scraper, Playwright, video recording, storyboard, Datasette, coding agents, GPT-5.5, automation, demo, open source

item.title: The AI Compass 摘要
item.description:
The AI Compass is a political compass-style quiz with 29 questions about AI and AI ethics, categorizing users into one of 30 archetypes. The author's first attempt placed them as 'The Garage Tinkerer', with Simon Willison as patron saint. The quiz is implemented as a single-page React app using the script type='text/babel' trick to avoid a build step, with source code available on GitHub.
标签: AI, AI ethics, quiz, archetypes, React, single-page app, generative AI, LLMs, Simon Willison, open source

item.title: Three Players From the Japanese Men’s National Team vs. 100 School Children 摘要
item.description:
A 2018 video from Japan shows three players from the Japanese men's national soccer team playing against 100 school children. The clip is highlighted as an entertaining and impressive display, with the author noting it's the best soccer video they've seen in a long while, despite the ongoing World Cup action.
标签: soccer, Japan, World Cup, video, football, national team, school children, entertainment, sports

item.title: Distinguishing variables from parameters 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses the subtle but important distinction between variables and parameters in mathematics. While technically both are variables, parameters are fixed within a given context but vary across applications. The author explains how notation like semicolons (e.g., f(x; k)) or vertical bars (e.g., f(x | μ, σ)) helps convey this distinction, providing clarity about which arguments are integrated over and which are fixed. This pedagogical nuance aids understanding in fields like probability and special functions.
标签: mathematics, notation, variables, parameters, pedagogy, probability, special functions, education

item.title: ★ The Supreme Court Rules That Law Enforcement’s Use of ‘Geofence Warrant’ Was a ‘Search’ (But May Be Moot, Technically, Since 2024) 摘要
item.description:
The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 in Chatrie v. United States that law enforcement's use of a geofence warrant to obtain Google location data constitutes a Fourth Amendment search, requiring reasonableness review. The case involved a 2019 Virginia bank robbery where police used Google's Location History data to identify suspects. Justice Kagan emphasized reasonable expectation of privacy in cell phone location records. Google changed its data storage in December 2023 to on-device and encrypted, reducing geofence warrant susceptibility. Apple never collected such aggregate location data. The ruling sets precedent for digital privacy but may reinforce public misconceptions about tech surveillance.
标签: Supreme Court, Fourth Amendment, geofence warrant, privacy, Google, location data, digital rights, surveillance, Apple, SCOTUS

item.title: Writing an LLM from scratch, part 34a -- building a JAX training loop for an LLM training run 摘要
item.description:
The author describes building an LLM from scratch using JAX, Flax NNX, and Optax, based on notes from Sebastian Raschka's book. They adopted an outside-in approach: first creating a minimal training harness with a simple A-to-A model (embedding + linear projection, no transformers) to verify the loop works, then gradually adding full GPT-2 architecture. The training loop uses a run-based directory structure for configuration and checkpoints. The project aims to replicate GPT-2 small quality using only personal notes, with JAX chosen via Twitter poll.
标签: LLM, JAX, Flax, Optax, deep learning, training loop, GPT-2, from scratch, machine learning, AI

item.title: Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship in 6-3 Decision 摘要
item.description:
The Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship in a 6-3 decision, with conservative justices Gorsuch, Alito, and Thomas dissenting. The author argues that the 14th Amendment's plain language makes the dissenters' position indefensible and calls for court reform. The piece emphasizes that birthright citizenship is foundational to the U.S. citizenship system, as most citizens have no formal proof beyond being born in the country. The author criticizes the corruption and impunity on the Court, noting that even Trump-appointed justices formed part of the majority.
标签: Supreme Court, birthright citizenship, 14th Amendment, constitutional law, politics, US citizenship, judicial reform, conservative dissent, TPM, Daring Fireball

item.title: Supreme Court Agrees to Review Apple’s Petition Regarding Civil Contempt Finding in ‘Apple v. Epic Games’ 摘要
item.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court granted certiorari to Apple's petition in Apple v. Epic Games, limited to Question 1 regarding whether Apple could be held in civil contempt for violating the spirit of an injunction by charging commissions on external payments, even if the letter of the injunction did not explicitly forbid such commissions. Question 2, concerning the injunction's scope, was not taken up. The author notes the irony of the Court's daily orders being set in Lucida Sans Typewriter, contrasting with the dignified Century Schoolbook used for opinions.
标签: Supreme Court, Apple, Epic Games, civil contempt, injunction, antitrust, App Store, legal, technology, US law

item.title: Gnome 摘要
item.description:
Gnome is a menubar app for macOS and iOS that lets users quickly search and paste animated GIFs via a keyboard shortcut. It syncs a local GIF library across devices via iCloud or Dropbox, and also searches Klipy's online collection. The app requires no account or sign-in, costs $7 (web) or $8 (App Store), and includes an iOS Messages extension and optional system-wide keyboard. Designed by Lex Friedman, it emphasizes speed and simplicity, with a one-time purchase unlocking both platforms.
标签: Gnome, GIF, macOS, iOS, productivity, app, communication, menubar, keyboard shortcut, sync

item.title: What's new in Claude Sonnet 5 摘要
item.description:
Claude Sonnet 5 has been released by Anthropic, with performance close to Opus 4.8 at lower prices. Key changes include removal of sampling parameters (temperature, top_p, top_k), a 1M token context window, 128K max output tokens, and adaptive thinking enabled by default. Pricing matches Sonnet 4.6 ($3/$15 per million tokens) with an introductory discount, but a new tokenizer increases token count by ~30% for English text, effectively raising costs. The model is less capable at cyber tasks than Mythos 5, allowing similar safeguards to Opus 4.7/4.8.
标签: AI, Claude, Anthropic, LLM, model release, pricing, tokenizer, generative AI, API changes

item.title: Nano Banana 2 Lite 摘要
item.description:
Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite Image (Nano Banana 2 Lite), its fastest and cheapest image generation model. The model can create images from text prompts, demonstrated by generating a 'Where's Waldo'-style scene with a raccoon holding a ham radio. While the result was better than previous Nano Banana models, it had spelling errors in the generated text. The model is available via AI Studio and the Gemini API.
标签: google, ai, generative-ai, llms, gemini, text-to-image, llm-release, nano-banana, image-generation, deepmind
